maxContractTerm.value: No statutory maximum found. Oregon law does not set a hard cap on the length of a school district's contract with a private student-transportation contractor. The school code (ORS Chapter 332; ORS 327.043) is silent on contract term. The Oregon Public Contracting Code (ORS Chapters 279A and 279B) imposes no general maximum term for goods or services contracts. ORS 279A.140(2)(c) - directed in terms to state-agency procurement - provides that 'the department or a state agency may enter into a public contract for any period of time, provided that the term of the contract and conditions of renewal or extension are included in the solicitation'; no parallel ORS provision sets a maximum term for local contracting agencies. School districts are 'local contracting agencies' (ORS 279A.010) and are governed by either the Attorney General's Model Rules (OAR Ch. 137, Divs. 46-49) or by their own rules adopted under ORS 279A.065(6), through their Local Contract Review Boards (ORS 279A.060). The five-year initial term + renewal-option pattern commonly seen in Oregon school-bus awards reflects district practice and the OAR 137-047-0800 amendments framework, not a state statutory cap.
maxContractTerm.citation: ORS 332.075 (powers of district school board, including authority to contract); ORS 332.405 (board duty to provide transportation); ORS 327.043 (when district required to provide transportation) - all silent on contract term. ORS 279A.025(1) (Public Contracting Code applies to all public contracting except listed exemptions, none of which carve out school transportation). ORS 279A.140(2)(c) (state-agency public contracts may be for any period of time provided term/renewal disclosed in the solicitation). No section in ORS Chapter 279B imposes a maximum term in years on a local contracting agency's contract for goods or services. OAR 137-047-0800 uses a one- to five-year example only to illustrate that amendments cannot exceed the term allowed in the solicitation; it is not a state-imposed cap.

rebidRequired.value: Conditional. Oregon school districts are 'local contracting agencies' subject to the Public Contracting Code (ORS 279A.025(1); ORS 279A.010). Award of a public contract for goods or services (including student transportation) must be made by one of the source-selection methods in ORS 279B.050 - competitive sealed bidding (ORS 279B.055) or competitive sealed proposals/RFP (ORS 279B.060) - unless an exemption applies (small procurements ORS 279B.065 [<= $25,000], intermediate procurements ORS 279B.070 [> $25,000 and <= $250,000], sole-source ORS 279B.075, emergency ORS 279B.080, or special procurements ORS 279B.085). At expiration of the awarded term (including any renewal options identified in the original solicitation), the district must run a new competitive process under ORS 279B.050 unless one of those statutory exemptions or a cooperative procurement under ORS 279A.200-.225 applies. Neither ORS Chapter 332 (school administration) nor ORS Chapter 327 (state school fund / transportation) imposes a transportation-specific rebid rule that differs from the general Public Contracting Code requirement.
rebidRequired.citation: ORS 279B.050 (methods of source selection - sealed bid under 279B.055 or sealed proposals under 279B.060 unless exempt); ORS 279B.055; ORS 279B.060; ORS 279B.065 (small procurements); ORS 279B.070 (intermediate procurements); ORS 279B.075 (sole-source); ORS 279B.080 (emergency); ORS 279B.085 (special procurements); ORS 279A.025(1) (Code applies to all public contracting except listed exemptions); ORS 279A.060 (Local Contract Review Boards); ORS 279A.065 (Model Rules / agency may adopt its own rules); ORS 279A.200-.225 (cooperative procurements).

renewalMechanics.value: Renewal/extension is permitted, but only on the terms disclosed in the original solicitation. Under OAR 137-047-0800 (Attorney General Model Rule applicable to contracting agencies that have not adopted their own rules under ORS 279A.065(6)), a contracting agency may amend a contract without further competition where the amendment is within the scope of the procurement as described in the solicitation documents and the amended contract 'does not have a total term greater than allowed in the Solicitation Documents... after combining the initial and extended terms.' The rule's illustrative example states: 'a one-year Contract described as renewable each year for up to four additional years, may be renegotiated as a two to five-year Contract, but not beyond a total of five years.' ORS 279A.140(2)(c) (state-agency context) likewise requires the term and conditions of any renewal or extension to be included in the solicitation. Districts that adopt their own LCRB rules under ORS 279A.065(6) typically replicate this approach in OSBA-modeled DJC / DJC-AR board policies (e.g., Beaverton, Reynolds, Bend-La Pine, Oregon City), which permit renewals only as authorized in the original solicitation or contract.
renewalMechanics.citation: OAR 137-047-0800 (Amendments to Contracts and Price Agreements); ORS 279A.140(2)(c) (term and conditions of renewal/extension included in solicitation - state agency context); ORS 279A.065 (Model Rules vs. agency-adopted rules); OSBA-modeled school district DJC / DJC-AR policies citing ORS 279A/B/C and OAR Ch. 137.

statutoryCitation.value: Primary authority - Oregon Public Contracting Code: ORS 279A.010 (definitions of 'contracting agency' and 'local contracting agency'); ORS 279A.025(1) (Code applies to all public contracting except listed exemptions); ORS 279A.060 (Local Contract Review Boards); ORS 279A.065 (AG Model Rules apply to a contracting agency that has not adopted its own; agency may adopt its own); ORS 279A.140 (contract period - state-agency procurement; the only ORS provision that affirmatively allows 'any period of time, provided that the term... and conditions of renewal or extension are included in the solicitation'); ORS 279B.050-.085 (source-selection methods - sealed bidding, RFP, small, intermediate, sole-source, emergency, and special procurements); ORS 279A.200-.225 (cooperative procurements). School authority: ORS 332.075 (powers of district school board, including power to contract and to delegate to superintendent, with carve-outs for collective bargaining agreements and service contracts including labor performed by district employees); ORS 332.405 (board duty to provide pupil transportation); ORS 327.043 (when a district is required to provide transportation - elementary > 1 mile, secondary > 1.5 miles). Administrative authority: OAR Chapter 137, Division 47 (AG Model Rules for public procurements of goods or services), especially OAR 137-047-0800 (Amendments to Contracts and Price Agreements). Pupil-transportation operational/safety rules: OAR Chapter 581, Division 53 (School Bus Driver Training, Licensing, and School Bus Standards), including OAR 581-053-0004 requiring transportation entities that contract out pupil transportation to ensure contractor compliance with applicable laws and rules. No transportation-specific Oregon statute or rule sets a contract-term cap, mandatory rebid interval, or renewal mechanic separate from the Public Contracting Code framework.

districtVariance.value: Yes. ORS 279A.065(6) expressly authorizes a contracting agency (including a school district acting through its Local Contract Review Board under ORS 279A.060) to opt out of the Attorney General's Model Rules and adopt its own rules of procedure for public contracts, provided those rules 'specifically state that the model rules the Attorney General adopts under this section do not apply to the contracting agency.' Individual Oregon school districts therefore publish their own LCRB / public-contracting rules and DJ / DJC / DJC-AR board policies that vary in matters such as dollar thresholds for direct, small, and intermediate procurements; renewal-term language in solicitations; required board approval levels (e.g., Beaverton SD, whose Board acts as LCRB and retains authority over special procurements and exemptions); and special-procurement classes adopted by the LCRB. Posted district policies (e.g., Beaverton SD DJC, Reynolds SD DJC and DJC-AR, Roseburg Public Schools 'Contracting Rules Division 46/47/48/49,' Bend-La Pine SD DJ-AR, Oregon City SD DJ) all cite ORS 279A/B/C and OAR Ch. 137 as the controlling framework while applying locally-adopted variations. The substantive choice of bidding method, renewal availability, and special-procurement exemptions is therefore made at the district level within the statutory framework.
districtVariance.citation: ORS 279A.065(6) (contracting agency may adopt its own rules); ORS 279A.060 (LCRB); ORS 332.075 (board approval of certain contracts; carve-outs for collective bargaining and labor-by-district-employees service contracts); district policy examples include Roseburg Public Schools 'Contracting Rules' (citing ORS 279A.065), Reynolds SD policies DJC and DJC-AR, Beaverton SD policy DJC.

specialEducation.differentRules: No - special-education transportation contracts in Oregon are subject to the same Public Contracting Code framework (ORS Chapters 279A and 279B) as regular-route transportation. Oregon's special-education statutes (ORS Chapter 343) and rules (OAR Chapter 581, Division 15) impose substantive duties to provide transportation as a related service under IDEA and require qualifications/licensing of providers, but they do not set different contract-term, rebid, or renewal rules for special-education transportation contracts. ORS 343.221 authorizes districts to contract for special education with another school district, ESD, approved private agency, or credentialed individual; the statute is silent on contract term and on procurement procedure. OAR 581-023-0040 (Approved Transportation Costs for Payments from the State School Fund) is a cost-accounting rule, not a procurement rule, and treats contracted transportation (including for students with IEPs) as an approved cost without imposing different contracting requirements.
specialEducation.explanation: Federal law (IDEA, 20 U.S.C. 1401(26); 34 C.F.R. 300.34) establishes transportation as a 'related service' the district must provide if needed for a child with a disability to benefit from special education, and the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act (42 U.S.C. 11431 et seq.) creates a duty to transport homeless students; these federal requirements affect the duty to transport, not the state procurement rules governing contracts with private student-transportation vendors. In Oregon, contracts for special-education or McKinney-Vento transportation services with private contractors are procured under the same ORS 279A/279B framework as regular routes, with the same source-selection methods (ORS 279B.050-.085), the same renewal mechanics (OAR 137-047-0800 or the district's own LCRB rule), and no special term cap.
specialEducation.citation: ORS 343.221 (special education required; permitted contracts for services); OAR 581-023-0040 (Approved Transportation Costs - State School Fund); ORS 279A.025(1) (Public Contracting Code applies to all public contracting except listed exemptions); ORS 279B.050-.085 (source-selection methods). Federal context: 20 U.S.C. 1401(26); 34 C.F.R. 300.34; 42 U.S.C. 11431 et seq.

maxContractTerm.note: Re-verified directly against the Oregon Legislature's 2025 Edition of ORS Chapters 279A, 279B, 327, and 332. ORS 279A.140(2)(c) verbatim ('the department or a state agency may enter into a public contract for any period of time, provided that the term of the contract and conditions of renewal or extension are included in the solicitation') is a state-agency rule with no parallel cap for local contracting agencies. ORS Chapter 279B was searched section by section for any year-cap on goods/services contracts and none was found. ORS 332.075, 332.405, and 327.043 confirmed silent on contract length. Disconfirming searches for 'not to exceed', 'five years', 'ten years', and 'multi-year' in connection with pupil transportation surfaced no transportation-specific cap.

rebidRequired.note: Re-opened ORS 279B.050 directly. Text confirms: 'a contracting agency shall award a public contract for goods or services by competitive sealed bidding under ORS 279B.055 or competitive sealed proposals under ORS 279B.060' except for procurements under ORS 279B.065, 279B.070, 279B.075, 279B.080 or 279B.085. ORS 279A.025(1) confirmed verbatim - the Code applies to all public contracting except the listed exemptions (none cover school transportation). 'Conditional' label correctly captures the available statutory exemptions and cooperative-procurement option.

renewalMechanics.note: OAR 137-047-0800 re-verified via official OARD page and public.law mirror; the 'one-year Contract... renewable each year for up to four additional years... not beyond a total of five years' illustrative example, and the 'amendment within the scope... total term greater than allowed in the Solicitation Documents' standard, both confirmed verbatim. ORS 279A.140(2)(c) confirmed verbatim. Disconfirming searches for CPI caps, mandatory fuel-adjustment language, mandatory state agency approval of school district renewals, and a max number of renewals turned up no transportation-specific statutory limit.

statutoryCitation.note: Every cited section was re-opened and confirmed against the Oregon Legislature's official 2025 Edition (ORS 279A, 279B, 327, 332, 343) and the OARD official text (OAR 137-047-0800; OAR 581-053-0004; OAR 581-023-0040). No transportation-specific statutory or administrative carve-out was located.

districtVariance.note: ORS 279A.065(5)-(6) re-verified verbatim from the legislature site - a contracting agency that has not adopted its own rules is subject to the AG Model Rules; a contracting agency may adopt its own rules that 'specifically state that the model rules... do not apply' and 'prescribe the rules of procedure.' Beaverton SD policy DJC re-opened and confirmed citing ORS 279/279A/279B/279C and OAR Chapter 137 Divisions 045-049 (and OAR Chapter 125 Divisions 246-249), with the Board acting as LCRB.

specialEducation.note: ORS 343.221 re-verified - authorizes contracting with another district, ESD, approved private agency, or licensed individual; silent on contract term and procurement procedure. OAR 581-023-0040 confirmed as a cost-accounting rule only. No separate Oregon procurement track for special-education transportation surfaced; the Public Contracting Code remains the controlling framework.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term - 'No statutory maximum found.'
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: An Oregon statute (transportation or general procurement) caps a school district's student-transportation contract at a specific number of years (e.g., 5, 10), or imposes mandatory annual renewal/termination.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Read ORS Ch. 279B section-by-section on legislature.oregon.gov for any year-cap on goods or services contracts. (2) Read ORS Ch. 332 (Local Administration of Education) and ORS 327.043 for transportation-specific term limits. (3) Web search: 'Oregon ORS school district transportation contract "not to exceed" "five years" "ten years" pupil transportation.' (4) Web search: 'Oregon school bus contract term limit statute ORS pupil transportation rebid renewal.' (5) Cross-checked ORS 279A.140(2)(c) for any companion local-agency cap.
challengeMatrix.result: Sustained the finding. ORS 279A.140(2)(c) explicitly allows 'any period of time' for state-agency contracts subject to solicitation disclosure; no parallel cap exists for local contracting agencies and no transportation-specific cap was located.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Oregon law either (a) flatly requires re-bidding at expiration regardless of any exception, or (b) flatly does not require competitive selection for school-bus contracts (because school districts have a transportation-specific carve-out).
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Re-opened ORS 279B.050 - confirmed sealed bid/RFP default with statutory exemptions in 279B.065-085. (2) Searched for any school-transportation-specific carve-out in ORS Ch. 332 and Ch. 327 - none. (3) Searched ORS 279A.200-.225 (cooperative procurement) - confirms an alternative path to bidding for goods/services where authorized. (4) Confirmed ORS 279A.025(1) does not exempt school transportation.
challengeMatrix.result: Sustained. 'Conditional' is the correct label because the Public Contracting Code's default of competitive selection has multiple statutory exemptions (sole-source, emergency, special-procurement, cooperative) and the LCRB may use those exemptions within statutory limits.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics - renewal allowed only as disclosed in solicitation per OAR 137-047-0800.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Oregon caps renewals by a separate rule (CPI cap, fuel-cap, mandatory state-agency approval, max number of renewals), or conversely permits open-ended evergreen renewals without solicitation disclosure.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Re-opened OAR 137-047-0800 (via official OARD and public.law mirror) - confirmed in-scope/total-term standard and the one-to-five-year example. (2) Re-opened ORS 279A.140(2)(c) - confirms solicitation disclosure requirement (state-agency level). (3) Searched OAR Ch. 137 Divs. 46-49 for CPI/fuel/approval renewal caps - none located. (4) Searched ORS 279B for renewal-frequency or board-approval add-ons for transportation - none.
challengeMatrix.result: Sustained. No add-on renewal cap; the 'in-scope of solicitation' standard governs.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Special-education transportation contracts follow the same ORS 279A/B framework.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Oregon has a separate procurement track or term cap for special-education transportation (e.g., under ORS Ch. 343 or OAR Ch. 581 Div. 15), or McKinney-Vento / IDEA federal law overrides ORS 279A/B at the contract-procurement level in Oregon.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Re-opened ORS 343.221 - verifies authorization to contract for special education but is silent on transportation-contract term and procurement procedure. (2) Re-opened OAR 581-023-0040 - confirms cost-accounting only. (3) Searched OAR Ch. 581 Div. 15 for a separate procurement rule - none found. (4) Searched for an Oregon-specific carve-out from ORS 279A/B for IEP transportation - none located.
challengeMatrix.result: Sustained. No separate Oregon procurement track for special-education transportation. Federal IDEA / McKinney-Vento set the duty to transport; ORS 279A/B sets how the contract is procured.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: District Variance - Yes; ORS 279A.065(6) authorizes opt-out from AG Model Rules.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Either no Oregon school district has in fact adopted its own rules (so variance is theoretical), or ORS 279A.065 actually limits opt-out so districts must use the Model Rules.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Re-opened ORS 279A.065 verbatim - confirmed subsection (5) (Model Rules apply if no own-rule adopted) and subsection (6) (a contracting agency may adopt its own rules that specifically displace the Model Rules). (2) Re-opened Beaverton SD policy DJC - confirmed it cites ORS 279/279A/279B/279C and OAR Ch. 137 Divs. 045-049 (plus OAR Ch. 125 Divs. 246-249), and that the Board acts as the LCRB and retains authority over special procurements/exemptions. (3) Cross-checked Reynolds SD DJC bidding-requirements page (per Stage 1 ledger) - on re-fetch returned HTTP 403, treated as supporting but not load-bearing.
challengeMatrix.result: Sustained. Variance is real and on-the-books; districts vary in dollar thresholds, special-procurement classes, and renewal-language conventions within the statutory framework.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Currentness - Stage 1 cited '2023 Edition' of ORS.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A 2024, 2025, or 2026 session amendment changed contract-term, rebid, or renewal rules for school district student-transportation contracts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Re-opened ORS Ch. 279A and 279B on legislature.oregon.gov - the chapter headers now read '2025 EDITION'; ORS 279B notes that sections were amended or repealed during the 2026 regular session (table at 2026 A&R Tables). (2) Reviewed 2025 Legislative Summary - Transportation report and ODOT 2025 Legislative Summary - no school district student-transportation procurement amendments. (3) Confirmed the 2023 session change to small/intermediate thresholds ($25K / $250K, effective 1/1/2024) is reflected; that change does not alter term, renewal, or rebid timing.
challengeMatrix.result: Partially sustained - substantive findings hold, but code-currency label corrected to 2025 Edition with a note that 2026 session amendments are folded in / under way. No substantive 2024-2026 amendment touches term, rebid, or renewal for school district student-transportation contracts.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: No change to substantive confidence (High). Currency label updated.

sourcesReverified.note: Definition of 'local contracting agency' confirmed verbatim: 'a local government or special government body authorized by law to conduct a procurement.'
Verbatim: 'Except as provided in subsections (2) to (4) of this section, the Public Contracting Code applies to all public contracting.' None of the listed exemptions reaches school transportation.
Verbatim: 'If the governing body of a local contracting agency takes no action to provide otherwise, the governing body is the local contract review board.'
Subsections (5) and (6) verbatim confirmed - Model Rules apply if no own-rule adopted; agency may adopt own rules that displace Model Rules and prescribe procedure.

Verbatim: 'the department or a state agency may enter into a public contract for any period of time, provided that the term of the contract and conditions of renewal or extension are included in the solicitation.'
Verbatim confirmed. Sealed bid (279B.055) or sealed proposals (279B.060) default; exemptions in 279B.065, .070, .075, .080, .085.

Pathfinder cross-check confirms - no contract-term cap; carve-outs for collective bargaining agreements and service contracts including labor performed by district employees (those remain with the board).
Pathfinder cross-check confirms board duty to provide pupil transportation; silent on contract length and procurement.

sourcesReverified.citation: ORS 327.043 (when district required to provide transportation)
sourcesReverified.note: Verbatim - elementary > 1 mile; secondary > 1.5 miles; silent on procurement/contract term.
Pathfinder cross-check confirms authorization to contract for special education with district, ESD, approved private agency, or credentialed individual; silent on contract term/procurement.

sourcesReverified.citation: OAR 137-047-0800 (Amendments to Contracts and Price Agreements)
sourcesReverified.note: In-scope-of-solicitation standard, total-term limit, and one-to-five-year example confirmed verbatim via public.law mirror (the official OARD division page lists Division 47 Model Rules and indexes this rule).
Confirmed contractor-compliance language; safety/operational only - no contract-term or procurement rule.
Confirmed cites ORS 279/279A/279B/279C and OAR Ch. 125 Divs. 246-249 and OAR Ch. 137 Divs. 045-049; Board acts as LCRB.

Server returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch. Page exists per indexing and Stage 1 ledger; downgraded to indirect support for district-variance assertion but variance is independently supported by ORS 279A.065(6) and the Beaverton DJC re-verification.
PDF binary not parseable through WebFetch; not load-bearing - district-variance assertion supported by ORS 279A.065(6) and Beaverton DJC verification.
Confirmed silent on contract term, rebid, and renewal - does not impose a procurement rule distinct from ORS 279A/B.

sourceLedger.citation: ORS Chapter 279A - Public Contracting - General Provisions (whole chapter)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: 2025 Edition. The chapter header on the legislature site reads '2025 EDITION.' The legislature publishes ORS in odd-year editions; 2026 session laws (where applicable) appear in the 2026 A&R Tables and will fold into the next biennial edition.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics; Statutory Citation; District Variance
sourceLedger.relevantText: ORS 279A.010 defines 'contracting agency' as 'a public body authorized by law to conduct a procurement,' and 'local contracting agency' as 'a local government or special government body authorized by law to conduct a procurement. Local contracting agency includes any person authorized by a local contracting agency to conduct a procurement on behalf of the local contracting agency.' ORS 279A.025(1): 'Except as provided in subsections (2) to (4) of this section, the Public Contracting Code applies to all public contracting.' ORS 279A.060: 'If the governing body of a local contracting agency takes no action to provide otherwise, the governing body is the local contract review board of that local contracting agency.' ORS 279A.065(5)-(6): a contracting agency that has not adopted its own rules is subject to the Model Rules; a contracting agency 'may adopt the contracting agency's own rules of procedure for public contracts that... [s]pecifically state that the model rules the Attorney General adopts under this section do not apply to the contracting agency; and... [p]rescribe the rules of procedure.' ORS 279A.140(2)(c): 'Unless otherwise provided by law, the department or a state agency may enter into a public contract for any period of time, provided that the term of the contract and conditions of renewal or extension are included in the solicitation.'

sourceLedger.citation: ORS Chapter 279B - Public Contracting - Public Procurements (whole chapter)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: 2025 Edition. The chapter header reads '2025 EDITION' and the chapter notes that ORS sections in this chapter were amended or repealed by the Legislative Assembly during its 2026 regular session (see 2026 A&R Tables). The substantive sections quoted here were not changed in a manner that affects contract term, rebid, or renewal.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics; Statutory Citation
sourceLedger.relevantText: ORS 279B.050(1)-(3): 'Except as provided in subsection (2) of this section, a contracting agency shall award a public contract for goods or services by competitive sealed bidding under ORS 279B.055 or competitive sealed proposals under ORS 279B.060. (2) The requirements of subsection (1) of this section do not apply to public contracts established as provided in ORS 279B.065, 279B.070, 279B.075, 279B.080 or 279B.085. (3) Notwithstanding the applicability of ORS 279B.065, 279B.070, 279B.075, 279B.080 or 279B.085 to a public contract, a contracting agency nevertheless may award the public contract under subsection (1) of this section.' No section in Chapter 279B sets a maximum contract term in years.

sourceLedger.citation: ORS Chapter 332 - Local Administration of Education (esp. ORS 332.075 powers of board; ORS 332.405 transportation duty)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Statutory Citation; Max Contract Term (silence); Rebid Required (silence)
sourceLedger.relevantText: ORS 332.075 grants the district school board power to enter into contracts and to authorize the superintendent (by resolution or policy) to enter into and approve payment on contracts for products, materials, supplies, capital outlay, equipment and services within board-made appropriations - but expressly carves out 'collective bargaining agreements' and 'service contracts that include the provision of labor performed by employees,' both of which must remain with the board. ORS 332.405 establishes the board's duty to provide transportation but is silent on contract length and procurement.

sourceLedger.citation: ORS 327.043 - When district required to provide transportation; supplemental plans and waivers
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Statutory Citation (duty to transport - not contract term)
sourceLedger.relevantText: ORS 327.043(2): 'A school district is required to provide transportation for elementary school students who reside more than one mile from school and for secondary school students who reside more than 1.5 miles from school. A school district is also required to provide transportation for any student identified in a supplemental plan approved by the State Board of Education.' Silent on procurement and contract term.

sourceLedger.citation: ORS 343.221 - Special education required; permitted contracts for services
sourceLedger.relevantText: Authorizes districts to 'contract for special education... with another school district,' 'with an education service district,' 'with private agencies or organizations approved by the State Board of Education,' or 'for the provision of related services by a person in private practice if that person is registered, certified or licensed.' Silent on contract term and procurement procedure for transportation.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 2 - state administrative code (official Secretary of State)
sourceLedger.citation: OAR Chapter 137, Division 47 - Model Rules: Public Procurements for Goods or Services (esp. OAR 137-047-0800 Amendments to Contracts and Price Agreements)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Current as published by the Oregon Secretary of State OARD. Division 47 most recent permanent amendments effective 2019-2024; the rule has been periodically revised by the AG as part of biennial post-session reviews.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Renewal Mechanics; Max Contract Term (illustrative example only)
sourceLedger.relevantText: OAR 137-047-0800: a contracting agency may amend a contract without competition where (i) the amendment 'is within the scope of the Procurement as described in the Solicitation Documents' and (ii) 'the amended Contract does not have a total term greater than allowed in the Solicitation Documents... after combining the initial and extended terms.' Example: 'a one-year Contract described as renewable each year for up to four additional years, may be renegotiated as a two to five-year Contract, but not beyond a total of five years.'

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 2 - state administrative code (official Secretary of State)
sourceLedger.citation: OAR Chapter 581, Division 53 - School Bus Driver Training, Licensing, and School Bus Standards (esp. OAR 581-053-0004 General)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Current as published by the Oregon Secretary of State OARD.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Special-education differentiation (no different contracting rule); Statutory Citation
sourceLedger.relevantText: OAR 581-053-0004: 'Transportation entities that contract out all or part of their pupil transportation services are required to ensure that their contractor complies with all applicable laws and administrative rules.' Safety and operational compliance only; no contract-term or procurement rule.

sourceLedger.citation: OAR 581-023-0040 - Approved Transportation Costs for Payments from the State School Fund
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Current as published by the Oregon Secretary of State OARD.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Special-education differentiation (cost-accounting only - no different contracting rule)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Defines approved transportation costs (including 'All Contracted Transportation - Payments to parents and independent public or private contractors for transporting pupils'); cost-accounting rule only.

sourceLedger.citation: Oregon Department of Education - Pupil Transportation and Fingerprinting page; Oregon Pupil Transportation Manual (2018 Draft Final)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: OPTM 2018 Draft Final (current edition); ODE pages accessed 2026-06-24.
sourceLedger.relevantText: ODE organizes Division 53 OAR safety/operational requirements and the OPTM but does not impose contract-term or procurement rules distinct from ORS 279A/B and OAR Ch. 137. ODE acknowledges that transportation entities that contract out pupil transportation services must ensure contractor compliance with applicable laws and administrative rules.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 5 - school district policy / LCRB rule (variance evidence only)
sourceLedger.citation: Beaverton School District policy DJC (district policy citing ORS 279/279A/B/C and OAR Ch. 137 Divs. 045-049; Board acts as LCRB)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Posted current as of access date.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Cites ORS Chapter 279, 279A, 279B, 279C and OAR Ch. 125 Divs. 246-249 and OAR Ch. 137 Divs. 045-049 as legal references; Board acts as the LCRB and 'has made the findings necessary to support each special procurement... request.' Superintendent or designee authorized to approve emergency procurements and determine sole-source within board-set parameters.

sourceLedger.citation: Reynolds School District policy DJC and DJC-AR; Roseburg Public Schools 'Contracting Rules Division 46/47/48/49' (adopted under ORS 279A.065)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Both districts publish own-rule frameworks under ORS 279A.065(6) and cite ORS 279A/B/C and OAR Ch. 137. (Reynolds page returned HTTP 403 on re-verification; Roseburg PDF was a binary fetch - both treated as supporting but not load-bearing; district-variance assertion stands on ORS 279A.065(6) + Beaverton DJC verification.)

verificationNotes.searchStrategy: Two-phase. Phase 1: re-open every Stage 1 citation directly against the official Oregon Legislature site (oregonlegislature.gov) for ORS, against the Secretary of State OARD for administrative rules, and against the cited district policy pages. Phase 2: run disconfirming searches - (a) for any transportation-specific term cap or rebid interval, (b) for any school-bus carve-out from the Public Contracting Code, (c) for any separate special-education procurement rule, (d) for 2024-2026 session-law amendments to ORS 279A/B that touch term, rebid, or renewal - using firecrawl_search, WebFetch, and WebSearch. The Oregon legislature returned the ORS chapter pages, allowing verbatim verification of ORS 279A.010, 279A.025, 279A.060, 279A.065, 279A.140, 279B.050, 332.075, 332.405, 327.043, and 343.221.

verificationNotes.codeCurrency: Oregon Revised Statutes 2025 Edition is the most recent codified edition published by the Oregon Legislative Counsel Committee, as displayed on the legislature's official chapter pages ('2025 EDITION' header on each chapter). Intervening 2026 regular-session amendments to Chapter 279B are catalogued in the 2026 A&R Tables and will be folded into the next biennial edition; none touches contract-term, rebid, or renewal rules for school district student-transportation contracts. (Stage 1 had labeled '2023 Edition' - corrected here.)
verificationNotes.adminCodeCurrency: Oregon Administrative Rules - current as published in the official Oregon Secretary of State Administrative Rules Database (OARD), accessed 2026-06-24. OAR Chapter 137 Division 47 (AG Model Rules), OAR Chapter 581 Division 53 (ODE pupil-transportation safety/operational rules), OAR 581-023-0040, and OAR 581-053-0004 reviewed as published.
verificationNotes.sessionLawCheck: Reviewed the 2024 regular session, 2025 regular session, and 2026 regular session of the Oregon Legislative Assembly for any session law amending ORS Chapter 279A, 279B, 332, 327, or 343 in a manner that would create a transportation-specific contract-term cap, mandatory rebid interval, or renewal rule for school district student-transportation contracts. None located. ORS 279B chapter page notes 2026 session amendments but none alter the operative source-selection or term framework relevant to these fields. The 2023 amendments raised small-procurement direct-award to $25,000 and intermediate to $250,000 (effective 1/1/2024) - these are reflected in current text and do not alter term, renewal, or rebid timing.
negativeSearchPaths: Searched ORS Chapter 332 (Local Administration of Education) section-by-section for a transportation contract-term or rebid provision - none found.
Searched ORS Chapter 327 (State School Fund) for transportation-specific contracting rules - ORS 327.043 imposes duty to transport, silent on contract term/procurement.
Searched ORS Chapter 279B for any provision imposing a maximum term in years on a local contracting agency's goods/services contract - none found.
Searched ORS Chapter 343 (Special Education) for differentiated procurement or term rule for special-education transportation - ORS 343.221 authorizes contracting but is silent on term/procurement.
Searched OAR Chapter 581 Division 53 for any contract-term or procurement rule - none found; the division is safety/operational only.
Searched OAR Chapter 581 Division 23 (Transportation Costs - State School Fund) for any procurement rule - OAR 581-023-0040 is cost-accounting only.
Searched for an Oregon Attorney General opinion specifically interpreting ORS 279A/B in the school-bus contracting context - none located in publicly available official AG opinions.

Searched OAR Chapter 137 Divisions 46-49 (AG Model Rules) for a transportation-specific term cap or renewal cap (CPI, fuel-adjustment, max-renewals, mandatory state approval of district renewals) - OAR 137-047-0800 is the only term-related provision and limits amendments to what the solicitation allowed.
Searched the 2024, 2025, and 2026 Oregon legislative sessions for any amendment creating a transportation-specific contracting rule - none located.
verificationNotes.conflictChecks: No authoritative source-conflict identified. The Public Contracting Code (general procurement) and ORS Chapters 332/327/343 (school authority and duties) operate in parallel - Code controls procurement procedure; school chapters set duty to transport. OAR 137-047-0800 controls non-solicitation amendments and is consistent with ORS 279A.140(2)(c)'s solicitation-disclosure requirement for state agencies. District-level rules adopted under ORS 279A.065(6) are statutorily authorized variance, not conflict.
